name: Cascada de la Cimbarra and Charco del Negrillo - circular trailrunning through the natural site cascada del Cimbarra
name_original: Cascada de la Cimbarra y charco del Negrillo - circular trailrunning por el paraje natural cascada del Cimbarra
region_intro: Jogging routes around Aldeaquemada traverse the diverse landscapes of the Eastern Sierra Morena mountain range in Jaén, Spain. The region features varied terrain, including rugged slopes, Mediterranean forests, and riparian woodlands along streams. Joggers can explore areas characterized by impressive waterfalls, deeply entrenched gorges, and rolling countryside. Best jogging routes around Aldeaquemada The most popular jogging route is Cascada de la Cimbarra and Charco del Negrillo - circular trailrunning through the natural site cascada del Cimbarra, a 2.7 miles (4.3 km) trail that takes about 30 minutes to complete. This easy route offers scenic views of the iconic Cascada de la Cimbarra waterfall and other streams. Another top favourite among local runners is Presa de Valdeazores – Cerro de los Órganos loop from Río Despeñaperros, a difficult 9.8 miles (15.7 km) path. This route features significant elevation gain through the Sierra Morena mountains, providing a challenging running experience. Local runners also love the Valdeazores Chestnut Grove Trail loop from Parque Natural Despeñaperros, a 4.7 miles (7.6 km) trail leading through diverse forests and a chestnut grove, often completed in about 1 hour. Jogging around Aldeaquemada is defined by its impressive waterfalls, tranquil streams, and the varied terrain of the Sierra Morena mountains. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy paths to more challenging running trails. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 80 runners have used komoot to explore Aldeaquemada's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in Aldeaquemada?
A: There are over 8 running routes around Aldeaquemada, offering a variety of options for different fitness levels. These trails traverse the diverse landscapes of the Eastern Sierra Morena mountain range.

Q: Are there running routes suitable for beginners in Aldeaquemada?
A: Yes, Aldeaquemada offers several easy running routes perfect for beginners or those looking for a relaxed jog. An excellent option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e989169232/cascada-de-la-cimbarra-and-charco-del-negrillo-circular-trailrunning-through-the-natural-site-cascada-del-cimbarra" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cascada de la Cimbarra and Charco del Negrillo - circular trailrunning&lt;/a&gt;, which is 4.3 km long and takes about 30 minutes to complete, offering scenic views of the iconic waterfall.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the running trails in Aldeaquemada?
A: The running trails in Aldeaquemada feature diverse terrain, from rugged slopes and deeply entrenched gorges to Mediterranean forests and riparian woodlands. You'll encounter areas with abundant pines, oaks, and other trees, as well as geological formations of slate and quartzite, characteristic of the Sierra Morena mountains.

Q: Can I find running routes that feature waterfalls?
A: Absolutely! Aldeaquemada is renowned for its impressive waterfalls. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e989169232/cascada-de-la-cimbarra-and-charco-del-negrillo-circular-trailrunning-through-the-natural-site-cascada-del-cimbarra" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cascada de la Cimbarra and Charco del Negrillo - circular trailrunning&lt;/a&gt; route takes you past the magnificent 40-meter high Cascada de la Cimbarra and the beautiful &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5289848" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Charco del Negrillo Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any viewpoints along the jogging routes in Aldeaquemada?
A: Many trails in Aldeaquemada offer stunning panoramic viewpoints. You can find several miradores, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5620563" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Balcón de Despeñaperros Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5418434" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Desfiladero Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;, and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4215341" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador de los Órganos&lt;/a&gt;, providing breathtaking vistas of the natural surroundings and gorges.

Q: Are there challenging running trails for experienced runners?
A: Yes, for experienced runners seeking a challenge, Aldeaquemada offers difficult routes with significant elevation gain. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31857102" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Presa de Valdeazores – Cerro de los Órganos loop from Río Despeñaperros&lt;/a&gt; is a demanding 15.7 km path through the Sierra Morena mountains, perfect for a rigorous running experience.

Q: What is the best time of year to go running in Aldeaquemada?
A: The best time to visit Aldeaquemada for running is generally during spring and autumn when temperatures are milder. After rainy periods, the waterfalls are particularly spectacular. Summer can be hot, while winter offers a different, often tranquil, experience.

Q: Are there circular running routes in the area?
A: Yes, Aldeaquemada features several circular running routes. A popular easy option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e989169232/cascada-de-la-cimbarra-and-charco-del-negrillo-circular-trailrunning-through-the-natural-site-cascada-del-cimbarra" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cascada de la Cimbarra and Charco del Negrillo - circular trailrunning&lt;/a&gt;. For a moderate challenge, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38249707" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valdeazores Chestnut Grove Trail loop from Parque Natural Despeñaperros&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the running trails in Aldeaquemada?
A: While the region is very natural, specific regulations for dogs on trails can vary. It's generally advisable to keep dogs on a leash, especially in natural parks and areas with wildlife, to protect both your pet and the local ecosystem. Always check local signage for any specific restrictions.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural sights to see while running?
A: Yes, Aldeaquemada offers a unique blend of nature and history. Along some routes, you can discover more than 20 prehistoric rock art sites, which are a UNESCO World Heritage site, adding a cultural dimension to your outdoor activity.

Q: What kind of wildlife might I encounter on the trails?
A: The diverse ecosystems of the Sierra Morena support a rich array of wildlife. Joggers might encounter otters, deer, wild boar, foxes, and genets. The area is also a Special Bird Protection Area, home to numerous birds of prey like the endangered Spanish imperial eagle, griffon vulture, and eagle owl.

Q: Is there public transport access to the trailheads around Aldeaquemada?
A: Public transport options directly to specific trailheads in Aldeaquemada can be limited due to its rural mountain location. It's often recommended to travel by car to access the starting points of many running routes. Check local bus schedules for connections to Aldeaquemada village itself.
tips: Viewpoint with the best panoramic views of the Cimbarra waterfall and the entire natural area. The Cimbarra waterfall, approximately 40 meters high, which when it carries water creates a spectacular waterfall worth photographing.
A beautiful waterfall that has sculpted a beautiful natural lagoon at its feet, forming as a whole a spectacular point where it stops and rests a bit.

waypoints.name: Viewpoint of Cascada de la Cimbarra
